name: v1.1.5 injection-defense completeness + flaky-test elimination
summary: Security + reliability PATCH on the behavioral-correctness line. SECURITY: closes two prompt-injection-defense deferrals. (1) injection-output-cap-fail-loud (#113): the v1.1.3 input DoS cap (256KB) also governed the L4 output scan, so a legitimately large clean tool/LLM OUTPUT was scored as a maximal threat and either mislabeled '[Tool output filtered: potential injection detected]' or silently emptied; output now has a separate 1MiB cap and a bounded-prefix scan (clean large output passes, prefix injection still caught, oversized emits an honest tool_output_scan_truncated event), input stays fail-closed (deliberately asymmetric). (2) injection-positional-override-detection (#115): CheckInput matched 'ignore...previous' but not positional overrides ('ignore everything above', entity-encoded included), which scored 0 and reached the model unsanitized; a positional pattern now scores them caution so they are redacted (found by the adversarial exercise, root-caused deterministically). RELIABILITY: the flaky-test problem is eliminated wholesale and gated — an audit of all 64 test-sleep sites converted the flaky ones across nine packages to assert.Eventually polling (+ atomic watched-file writes, a load-invariant concurrency ratio, explicit eviction timestamps, a size-detectable keystore change), and a CI guard (scripts/check-test-sleeps.sh) blocks new time.Sleep-then-assert beyond the reviewed baseline (#116-#119). COVERAGE/PROCESS: adversarial-issue coverage to 0 gaps / 100% (#114), and the product-knowledge pack audit made a mandatory release-checklist item (#110). No new user-facing feature surface (that is v1.2.0, the packaged embedder). PRE-SHIP SOAK (2026-06-14, clone, cloud-provider-v4-flash, production quiesced): full 38-scenario battery 37/38, release_gate_findings=0, 22/23 families grade A; the one non-A (continuity_topic_anchor) is a documented verifier-sensitivity re-verified PASS 3/3 in isolation (variance, unrelated to the injection-path changes). SHIPPED (2026-06-14): promoted via PR #121 (develop->main, merge 66257224), tag v1.1.5; release workflow green, CI tree-reuse fired (Pre-release Validation reused the green develop push CI for tree 6798d9da — heavy validation steps skipped, third consecutive release validating the machinery); published live — the darwin-arm64 binary reports 1.1.5 clean on go1.26.4 (no -dev), SHA256SUMS.txt present, /releases/latest resolves v1.1.5 (prerelease=false), site release-sync dispatch succeeded (pipeline-owned deploy).
targetWindow: Patch release; formal cut after pre-ship behavioral soak per docs/testing/release-procedure.md
updateSource: PRs #110, #113, #114, #115, #116, #117, #118, #119 + artifacts #120 merged to develop; promoted via #121 and tag v1.1.5 shipped 2026-06-14; release workflow green with CI tree-reuse

workstreams.name: Injection-defense completeness + flaky-test elimination
workstreams.summary: Two injection-defense deferrals closed (output-cap fail-loud, positional-override detection), the flaky-test class eliminated and gated, adversarial coverage to 100%, and the product-knowledge audit made mandatory.

evidence.label: internal/agent green under -race; FuzzInjectionDetector_Sanitize clean (input DoS guard intact)
Flaky-test CI guard validated (clean tree passes; synthetic new test sleep fails)

required.title: Injection output-cap fails loud instead of silently dropping output
required.summary: Separate maxOutputScanBytes (1 MiB) + bounded-prefix scan in ScanOutput, so oversized clean tool/LLM output is no longer scored as a maximal threat and dropped/mislabeled; loop.go emits an honest tool_output_scan_truncated event on oversize; input-path cap stays fail-closed at 256KB. TDD RED->GREEN (oversized benign scored 1.0/blocked, SanitizeModelOutput emptied), internal/agent green under -race, fuzz clean. Closes the v1.1.3 deferral.

required.title: Detect positional instruction-override injections
required.summary: CheckInput gained a positional-override pattern (ignore (everything|all|the|any)? (above|below|preceding)) so positional-phrased injections — including HTML-entity-encoded, which CheckInput decodes before matching — score at least caution and are sanitized rather than reaching the model verbatim (they scored 0.0 before). Found by the adversarial exercise; root-caused deterministically from the pipeline injection-defense stage. TDD RED->GREEN, no clean-input false positives, -race + fuzz clean.

required.title: Eliminate flaky tests across nine packages and gate against new ones
required.summary: Audited all 64 test-sleep sites; converted the flaky sleep-then-assert ones to assert.Eventually polling (+ atomic watched-file writes, load-invariant concurrency ratio, explicit eviction timestamps, size-detectable keystore change) across nine packages; added scripts/check-test-sleeps.sh to the CI lint job to fail any new time.Sleep in tests beyond the reviewed baseline. Closes the recurring flaky-CI problem that broke #114/#115.
evidence: PRs #116, #117, #118 (sweeps); PR #119 (guard: scripts/check-test-sleeps.sh + scripts/test-sleeps-baseline.txt + ci.yml lint step)

required.title: Adversarial-issue coverage to 0 gaps / 100%
required.summary: Two new guard-edge regression scenarios; systemic corpus fix excluding deferred/unshipped bug-fixes until they ship; justified out-of-scope classifications for non-behavioral fixes (each citing a real gate).

required.title: Make the product-knowledge pack audit a mandatory release-checklist item
required.summary: Auditing/updating docs/product-knowledge/manifest.json + its source-backed docs is now a required item in docs/contributing.md release checklist and docs/testing/release-procedure.md preconditions.

deferred.title: Count grounding-judge-mediated guard retries in guard_retry_count
deferred.summary: Judge-mediated task_deferral retries do not increment guard_retry_count; the downstream empty-diagnosis symptom is already fixed (v1.1.4), but the counter itself is still inaccurate for RCA. Carried forward.

deferred.title: Honest blocker for tool-calling guard-exhaustion turns
deferred.summary: The canned 'After N attempts, we couldn't get an answer' fallback was observed (rare, ~4%) on a tool-calling vault-inspection turn during the v1.1.5 adversarial exercise — an uncovered guard-exhaustion path the v1.1.4 honest-blocker (introspection-only) did not handle. Could not be reliably reproduced this cycle (0/12 in a focused repro), so it cannot yet be root-caused from recorded state; carried as a roadmap item for a dedicated RCA when it can be captured.
